text: Strength training programs produce a large beneficial effect on running economy in highly trained middle- and long-distance runners.
population: competitive middle- and long-distance runners with VO2max >60 ml/kg/min

text: Strength training performed 2-3 times per week for 8-12 weeks is sufficient to improve running economy in highly trained runners.
population: competitive middle- and long-distance runners with VO2max >60 ml/kg/min

text: Low to moderate resistance training intensities (40-70% of one repetition maximum) are effective for improving running economy in highly trained runners.
population: competitive middle- and long-distance runners with VO2max >60 ml/kg/min

text: An effective strength training protocol for runners includes 2-4 resistance lower-body exercises plus up to 200 jumps and 5-10 short sprints per session.
population: competitive middle- and long-distance runners with VO2max >60 ml/kg/min

text: Combining resistance exercises with plyometric exercises is an appropriate strategy to improve running economy in highly trained runners.
population: competitive middle- and long-distance runners with VO2max >60 ml/kg/min
